stories.headline: Democrats up in Virginia, but US voters may pay price for redistricting war
stories.summary: Experts say unprecedented flurry of tit-for-tat congressional redistricting risks surge in gerrymandering.

stories.headline: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
stories.summary: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.

stories.headline: Navy Secretary John Phelan Is Leaving the Pentagon and the Trump Administration
stories.summary: John Phelan is leaving the Pentagon after months of tension with Pete Hegseth and other Pentagon leaders. The tumult comes as the Navy has been engaged in war with Iran.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Israel kills Lebanese journalist; Tehran-US talks stalled
stories.summary: Israeli attacks on Lebanon killed at least five people on Wednesday, including Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil.

stories.headline: US Navy chief leaving post 'effective immediately', Pentagon says
stories.summary: US Navy Secretary John Phelan is the latest high-ranking military leader to leave the administration in recent months.

stories.headline: Indonesia Sells Biggest Samurai Bond Since 2024 on Firm Demand
stories.summary: Indonesia raised ¥172.1 billion ($1.1 billion) in its biggest Samurai bond sale in two years as strong investor demand for its credit outweighed concerns about budgetary pressures due to the Middle East war.

stories.headline: Gotion Founder on US Expansion, Supply Chains, and Industry Competition
stories.summary: Gotion High-tech Founder Li Zhen tells Bloomberg about the battery maker's growth outlook. He also discusses US expansion plans, the Iran war's impact on supply chains, and fierce industry "involution." He spoke exclusively with Bloomberg's Minmin Low. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Kalshi Fines and Suspends 3 Political Candidates for Betting on Their Races
stories.summary: The prediction market said the candidates violated new rules. The platform and its competitors face growing scrutiny from lawmakers and regulators over how political betting is policed.

stories.headline: John Phelan forced out as Navy secretary after 13 months
stories.summary: The billionaire was a significant contributor to President Donald Trump’s campaign, but officials said he clashed repeatedly with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.

stories.summary: The poll is taking place against a controversy over a sweeping revision of electoral rolls.

stories.headline: Sadiq Khan: Labour risks being ‘stonked’ in London elections
stories.summary: Mayor says campaigning ahead of May 7 local elections is some of ‘most difficult’ he has experienced in more than 40 years

stories.summary: Idea long pursued by banking sector lobbyists faces opposition from regulators

stories.headline: Yuan to Surpass Yen in Currency Options Trading, LCH Says
stories.summary: The yuan is on track to overtake the yen as the second-most traded currency against the dollar in the foreign-exchange options market, according to London-based clearing house LCH.

stories.headline: Nestlé Sales Rise as Snacks and Coffee Outweigh Recall Hit
stories.summary: Nestlé SA showed resilient sales growth in the first quarter, helping the Swiss foodmaker contain the fallout from the largest product recall in its history.

stories.headline: Stock Futures Fall, Pound Weakens on Talks Uncertainty
SET President: IPO Pipeline Faces a Difficult Situation
stories.summary: Stock Exchange of Thailand President Asadej Kongsiri discussed the IPO pipeline, saying it is in a difficult situation. He also noted that the Thai exchange plans to introduce “mini-gold” futures. He spoke with Haslinda Amin on Bloomberg Insight. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Indonesia Rupiah Falls Most in Seven Months on Rising Oil Prices
stories.summary: The Indonesian rupiah declined by the most in seven months as rising oil prices hurt sentiment for the nation’s assets, as Bank Indonesia signal that they have increased intervention efforts.

stories.headline: Modi Seeks to Win Key India State After Voter Rolls Purged
stories.summary: Just two weeks before voters in India’s eastern state of West Bengal were due to go to the polls, about 9 million names were removed from the registered roll, causing an uproar among opposition groups.

stories.headline: Hyundai Motor Misses Estimates as Geopolitical Headwinds Hit
stories.summary: Hyundai Motor Co. first-quarter earnings missed estimates, as South Korea’s largest automaker grappled with US tariffs and cooling demand in key markets.

stories.headline: JPMorgan to Add Philippine Bonds to Key EM Index in 2027
stories.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. said it will add local-currency bonds from the Philippines into its widely followed emerging-market index early next year, a move that may draw foreign investment and help spur the nation’s economy.

stories.headline: Big Bet on Orban’s Exit Came From Center of His Family’s Empire
stories.summary: An asset-management fund in the Equilor group of companies part-owned by Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s son-in-law profited from financial-market bets on the strongman losing power.

stories.summary: Systematic sexual violence and harassment by Israeli soldiers, settlers displacing Palestinians in occupied West Bank.

stories.headline: Trump eyes Iran deal with many of the trade-offs he blasted Obama for accepting

stories.headline: How the War Powers Act Could Pressure Trump to End the Iran War
stories.summary: A decades-old law allows the president to wage war without congressional approval for 60 days, then limits his options for continuing. President Trump may seek to get around it.

stories.headline: Nigeria's suspected coup plotters deny treason charges
stories.summary: Allegations of a coup plot first surfaced last year when an Independence Day parade was cancelled.

stories.summary: The Pentagon assessment, shared in a classified briefing for lawmakers, suggests gasoline and oil prices could remain elevated through the midterm elections.

stories.headline: Top ministers quit after Peru’s president postpones F-16 fighter jet deal
stories.summary: The Trump administration has warned against Peru pulling out of the deal, saying it reflects 'bad faith' negotiations.

stories.headline: Prediction market Kalshi docks three US candidates for betting on own races
stories.summary: Penalties come amid calls for greater oversight; company pledges to proactively police 'insider trading' on platforms.

stories.headline: Man City topple Arsenal at Premier League summit with nervy win at Burnley
stories.summary: Manchester City relegate Burnley with 1-0 win to also take top spot from Arsenal in the Premier League title race.

stories.headline: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel
stories.summary: The impact is being felt by manufacturers, retailers and the digital sector, amid warnings it could get worse if the war resumes.

stories.headline: From Epstein to sock puppets: Key takeaways from Kevin Warsh's Fed confirmation hearing

stories.headline: Robot chases wild boars off the streets of Warsaw
stories.summary: The Polish capital has seen the number of wild boar on its streets soar to more than 3,000 - a 20-fold increase since 2020.

stories.headline: Ukraine family get cancer and bomb news on same day

stories.headline: House Democrat resigns ahead of possible expulsion vote over alleged financial crimes
stories.summary: Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick was indicted last year on charges that she stole and laundered federal disaster money and used it to support a congressional campaign.

stories.headline: Taiwan president blames China for forced cancellation of Eswatini trip

stories.headline: Trump says US will not lift Hormuz blockade until deal made with Iran
stories.summary: The US president's comments come amid uncertainty over whether Iran will attend peace talks in Pakistan this week.

stories.headline: Rumen Radev's party wins Bulgarian election
stories.summary: With 87% of the vote now counted, Progressive Bulgaria has secured a majority in parliament.

stories.headline: India has splurged billions on metro trains. But where are the commuters?
